overview.subtitle: What is the D.Pharma Programme?
paragraphs: The Diploma in Pharmacy (D.Pharma) is a career-oriented, two-year program approved by the Pharmacy Council of India (PCI). It serves as the gateway for students aiming to enter the pharmaceutical sector quickly. The course focuses on the fundamentals of preparing, dispensing, and the appropriate use of medications.

Students undergo rigorous training in well-equipped laboratories, learning everything from human anatomy and biochemistry to the legal regulations governing the sale of drugs. Upon successful completion, graduates are eligible to register as licensed pharmacists with the State Pharmacy Council.

scope.body: The scope of D.Pharma is vast and immediately rewarding. The primary benefit is the eligibility to obtain a drug license, allowing graduates to open and operate their own retail or wholesale chemist shops. Additionally, graduates are in high demand as Hospital Pharmacists in both private and government sectors, Medical Representatives (MR), and Production Technicians in pharmaceutical manufacturing units. It also serves as a direct entry point to the 2nd year of B.Pharma (Lateral Entry).
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the pharmaceutical industry

curriculum.subtitle: A PCI-structured curriculum for aspiring pharmacists
curriculum.introNote: The D.Pharma curriculum follows the strict guidelines laid down by the Education Regulations of the Pharmacy Council of India.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 500-hour practical training in a recognized hospital or pharmacy is required to complete the diploma.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: Unlike degree programs, D.Pharma generally operates on an Annual System (yearly exams) rather than semesters, as per PCI regulations. The first year builds the foundation of human anatomy and basic drug chemistry. The second year focuses on advanced pharmacology, hospital pharmacy operations, and drug laws.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Foundation Sciences
accordionSections.content: Pharmaceutics-I (Theory & Practical), Pharmaceutical Chemistry-I (Theory & Practical), Pharmacognosy (Theory & Practical), Biochemistry & Clinical Pathology (Theory & Practical), Human Anatomy & Physiology (Theory & Practical), Health Education & Community Pharmacy.

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Advanced Pharmacy & Laws
accordionSections.content: Pharmaceutics-II (Theory & Practical), Pharmaceutical Chemistry-II (Theory & Practical), Pharmacology & Toxicology (Theory & Practical), Pharmaceutical Jurisprudence, Drug Store and Business Management, Hospital and Clinical Pharmacy (Theory & Practical).

accordionSections.title: Mandatory Hospital Training
accordionSections.content: After passing the second-year exams, students must complete a mandatory practical training of not less than 500 hours spread over 3 months in a PCI-recognized government or corporate hospital/pharmacy.

admissionCriteria.text: Through Majority & Scholarship Test (DPM-SAT). For details,
Admission procedure: For follow-up admissions process. To apply,

If a student fails to maintain the required academic standards, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. The student must belong to the Science stream (Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Mathematics). Arts/Commerce students are not eligible.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for D.Pharma? Can Arts students apply?

items.a: D.Pharma is a 2-year program followed by a 3-month mandatory hospital training. The fee is ₹40,000 per year.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: Yes! D.Pharma is the minimum qualification required in India to register with the State Pharmacy Council and obtain a valid license to open a medical/chemist shop.
items.q: Will I get a license to open a medical store after this?

items.a: D.Pharma is a 2-year diploma focused on basic dispensing and retail pharmacy. B.Pharma is a 4-year degree focused on deep pharmacology, drug manufacturing, and R&D. If you want to study further, B.Pharma is better; if you want to start a business quickly, D.Pharma is ideal.
items.q: What is the difference between D.Pharma and B.Pharma?

items.a: Yes, after completing D.Pharma, you are eligible for Lateral Entry directly into the 2nd year of the B.Pharma degree program.
items.q: Can I do B.Pharma after completing D.Pharma?

items.a: Yes, you have to complete a rigorous 500-hour practical training spanning over 3 months in a recognized hospital. This is a mandatory PCI requirement to get your license.
items.q: Is hospital training compulsory?

items.a: Graduates work as Pharmacists in Govt/Private Hospitals, run their own chemist shops, work as Medical Representatives (MRs), or as inventory managers in pharmaceutical warehouses.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after D.Pharma?

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Pharma Programme?
paragraphs: The Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma) is a comprehensive four-year undergraduate degree designed to build elite pharmaceutical professionals. Unlike a diploma which focuses on dispensing, B.Pharma is deeply rooted in the scientific research, manufacturing, and quality control of medicinal drugs.

Approved by the Pharmacy Council of India (PCI), this program integrates extensive laboratory practice with theoretical knowledge. Students learn the entire lifecycle of a drug—from synthesizing chemical compounds and studying plant-based medicines to conducting clinical trials and ensuring regulatory compliance.

scope.body: The Indian pharmaceutical industry is the 'pharmacy of the world,' and the demand for B.Pharma graduates is massive. Graduates are aggressively recruited by top pharma giants (like Sun Pharma, Cipla, Dr. Reddy's) as Quality Control (QC) Analysts, Formulation Scientists, Clinical Research Associates (CRAs), and Regulatory Affairs Managers. It also opens elite government career paths such as Government Drug Inspector and Hospital Pharmacist. Furthermore, it is the primary stepping stone for pursuing an M.Pharma or an MBA in Pharma Management.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in global drug manufacturing and R&D

curriculum.subtitle: An elite PCI-structured curriculum for clinical excellence
curriculum.introNote: The B.Pharma curriculum is a rigorous 8-semester journey balancing deep chemical sciences with clinical biology and industrial operations.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory Industrial Training program inside a functioning pharmaceutical manufacturing plant is required before graduation.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 4-year B.Pharma program follows a strict semester system as mandated by the PCI. Year 1 covers foundational biology and basic chemistry. Year 2 & 3 dive into core pharma subjects like microbiology, pharmacology, and drug formulation. Year 4 focuses on advanced medicinal chemistry, clinical trials, novel drug delivery systems, and industrial training.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Foundation Sciences (Sem 1 & 2)
accordionSections.content: Human Anatomy & Physiology I & II, Pharmaceutical Analysis, Pharmaceutics I, Pharmaceutical Inorganic Chemistry, Communication Skills, Pathophysiology, Biochemistry, Environmental Sciences.

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Core Pharmacy (Sem 3 & 4)
accordionSections.content: Pharmaceutical Organic Chemistry II & III, Physical Pharmaceutics I & II, Pharmaceutical Microbiology, Pharmaceutical Engineering, Medicinal Chemistry I, Pharmacology I, Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry I.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: Advanced Pharmacology (Sem 5 & 6)
accordionSections.content: Medicinal Chemistry II & III, Pharmacology II & III, Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry II, Industrial Pharmacy I, Pharmaceutical Jurisprudence, Herbal Drug Technology, Biopharmaceutics and Pharmacokinetics.

accordionSections.title: Year-4: Industrial & Clinical Ops (Sem 7 & 8)
accordionSections.content: Instrumental Methods of Analysis, Industrial Pharmacy II, Pharmacy Practice, Novel Drug Delivery System, Biostatistics and Research Methodology, Social and Preventive Pharmacy, Pharma Marketing Management, Industrial Training, Final Project Work.

years.contentTitle: Mastering the Basics of Biology & Chemistry
years.contentDesc: The first year builds a robust foundation in human anatomy, biochemistry, and pharmaceutical inorganic chemistry.
The second year dives into complex organic chemistry, studying plant-based drugs (Pharmacognosy), and pharmaceutical microbiology.
The third year focuses heavily on how drugs interact with the human body (Pharmacology) and designing complex chemical drugs.
The final year is dedicated to clinical research, novel drug delivery systems, analytical testing using advanced machines, and industrial internships.

overview.subtitle: What is the Nutrition & Dietetics Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Sc. Nutrition and Dietetics program is an essential allied healthcare degree that addresses the growing global burden of lifestyle diseases like diabetes, obesity, and cardiovascular disorders. The course provides a deep understanding of human physiology, food microbiology, and therapeutic nutrition.

Students are trained not just in theoretical biology, but in practical clinical settings. Our state-of-the-art Dietetics Kitchen and Food Analysis labs empower students to formulate custom diet plans, analyze food adulteration, and understand public health nutrition, preparing them for roles in hospitals, sports academies, and the FMCG sector.

scope.body: As people become highly conscious of their health and fitness, the demand for certified dietitians has skyrocketed. Graduates can work as Clinical Dietitians in super-specialty hospitals (Apollo, Max, Fortis), Sports Nutritionists for athletic teams, Food Safety Auditors (FSSAI), and R&D executives in FMCG companies like Nestlé and Danone. Many graduates also establish successful independent wellness consulting practices.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in global wellness and healthcare

curriculum.subtitle: A science-driven curriculum for clinical wellness
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum perfectly balances core medical biology (anatomy, physiology) with advanced food science and therapeutic diet planning.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 6-week clinical internship in the dietary department of a recognized hospital is required to complete the degree.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 3-year program progresses from basic human biology in the first year to advanced therapeutic diet formulation in the second year. The final year focuses on clinical internships, food safety laws, and specialized branches like sports and pediatric nutrition.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Human Physiology I, Basics of Food Science, Nutritional Biochemistry I, Communication Skills, Public Health & Epidemiology. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Human Physiology II, Nutritional Biochemistry II, Family Meal Management, Food Microbiology, Environmental Science. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Clinical Nutrition I, Diet Therapy I, Food Preservation & Processing, Basic Dietetics Lab, Community Nutrition. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Clinical Nutrition II, Diet Therapy II, Food Quality Control & Analysis, Advanced Dietetics Lab, Research Methodology. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Sports & Fitness Nutrition, Pediatric & Geriatric Nutrition, Food Service Management, Elective I, Minor Project. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Food Laws & Regulations (FSSAI), Elective II, Mandatory Clinical Internship (Hospital Dietary Department), Final Viva-Voce. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Nutraceuticals and Functional Foods, Public Health Administration, and Entrepreneurship in Wellness.

bpt.title: Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPT)
bpt.eligibility: 10+2 in Science (PCB mandatory) with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
bpt.description: A highly specialized clinical degree focused on physical rehabilitation, movement science, and therapeutic exercises to treat patients suffering from physical dysfunctions or injuries.
bpt.metaTitle: Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPT) | Health Sciences | CPU
bpt.metaDescription: Pursue a 4.5-year BPT degree at Career Point University. Hands-on clinical training in our functional OPD, advanced rehab labs, and mandatory hospital internships.

hero.description: Heal through movement. Master the science of physical rehabilitation, orthopedics, and neurology in our state-of-the-art Physiotherapy OPD and medical labs.

accomplishments.heading: Department's Clinical Accomplishments & Impact
accomplishments.trustBadge: ✓ Advanced Clinical Training & Mandatory Hospital Internship

overview.subtitle: What is the BPT Programme?
paragraphs: The Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPT) is a rigorous four-and-a-half-year clinical degree program. It trains students in the art and science of physical medicine and rehabilitation, helping patients restore movement and function following injury, illness, or disability.

Our curriculum is heavily skewed towards practical clinical exposure. Starting from the second year, students spend significant hours in our fully functional, on-campus Physiotherapy OPD, treating real patients under the supervision of senior doctors. The program concludes with a mandatory 6-month clinical internship in a multi-specialty hospital.

scope.body: Physiotherapy is a highly respected and independent medical profession. Graduates can use the title 'Dr.' (suffix PT) and establish their own independent private clinics. Furthermore, they are aggressively hired by Multi-specialty Corporate Hospitals (Orthopedic & Neuro wards), Sports Teams & Fitness Academies, Rehabilitation Centers, and NGOs. It is also the necessary stepping stone for pursuing a Master of Physiotherapy (MPT) in specialized branches like Sports, Neurology, or Orthopedics.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in physical medicine and rehab

curriculum.subtitle: An intensive medical curriculum for rehabilitation experts
curriculum.introNote: The BPT curriculum mirrors the foundational subjects of an MBBS in the first two years, shifting heavily to physical therapies and clinical applications in the latter half.
curriculum.outroNote: A continuous 6-month rotatory internship in various hospital departments (Ortho, Neuro, ICU) is mandatory before the degree is awarded.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 4.5-year BPT program is intense. Year 1 builds a deep understanding of the human body. Year 2 introduces therapy modalities (Electro & Exercise). Year 3 & 4 focus entirely on specific medical conditions (Ortho, Neuro, Cardio) and their physiotherapeutic management. The final 6 months are spent purely in a hospital setting.

accordionSections.title: Year-1: Basic Medical Sciences (Sem 1 & 2)
accordionSections.content: Human Anatomy, Human Physiology, Biochemistry, Sociology & Psychology, Basic Nursing & First Aid, English Communication. Extensive Anatomy and Physiology Lab hours.

accordionSections.title: Year-2: Therapy Modalities (Sem 3 & 4)
accordionSections.content: Biomechanics & Kinesiology, Exercise Therapy, Electrotherapy, Pathology & Microbiology, Pharmacology, Clinical Observation in OPD.

accordionSections.title: Year-3: Clinical Specialties (Sem 5 & 6)
accordionSections.content: General Medicine, General Surgery, Orthopedics & Traumatology, Physiotherapy in Orthopedics, Community Medicine, Research Methodology.

accordionSections.title: Year-4: Advanced Rehab (Sem 7 & 8)
accordionSections.content: Neurology & Neurosurgery, Physiotherapy in Neurology, Cardio-Respiratory Diseases, Physiotherapy in Cardio-Respiratory, Sports Physiotherapy, Final Year Project.

accordionSections.title: 6-Month Compulsory Rotatory Internship
accordionSections.content: After passing the 8th semester, students must complete a continuous 6-month clinical internship rotating through Orthopedics, Neurology, ICU/Cardiology, and General Surgery wards in a recognized 100+ bedded hospital.

admissionCriteria.text: Through Majority & Scholarship Test (DPM-SAT). For details,
Admission procedure: For follow-up admissions process. To apply,

reasons.desc: With the rise in desk jobs and lifestyle issues, the demand for ergonomic consultants and physiotherapists is growing exponentially.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the BPT programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. The student must belong to the Science stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB) as mandatory subjects. Math students are not eligible.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Is Biology mandatory?

items.a: It is a 4.5-year degree program, which includes 4 years of academic coursework (8 semesters) followed by a 6-month mandatory clinical internship. The fee is ₹35,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: Yes, completing a continuous 6-month clinical internship in a recognized multi-specialty hospital is a strict mandatory requirement to obtain the degree.
items.q: Is the 6-month hospital internship compulsory?

items.a: Graduates can open their own independent physiotherapy clinics. They are also hired by corporate hospitals (Ortho/Neuro/ICU wards), Sports Academies, Rehabilitation Centers, and NGOs.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after this course?

items.a: Yes, as per professional norms in India, graduates holding a BPT degree can use the prefix 'Dr.' followed by the suffix '(PT)' to denote their specialization in Physiotherapy.
items.q: Can I use 'Dr.' before my name after completing BPT?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available?

items.a: Yes, starting from the second year, students are posted in the university's functional OPD where they observe and assist in treating real patients using electrotherapy and exercise therapy.
items.q: Will I get practical experience treating real patients?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the BPT programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from science student to clinical physiotherapist.

years.contentDesc: The first year builds an intense, medical-level foundation in human anatomy, physiology, and biochemistry.
The second year introduces the core tools of a physiotherapist: Biomechanics, Electrotherapy machines, and Exercise Therapy.
The third year focuses on understanding actual medical conditions like fractures, surgeries, and planning their specific rehab protocols.
The final phase covers complex Neurology, ICU (Cardio) rehab, Sports injuries, followed by a continuous 6-month hospital internship.

list.name: Vikram Singh
list.text: Working directly with patients in the university's Physiotherapy OPD from the second year gave me the clinical confidence I needed. The 6-month internship was the cherry on top.

list.name: Aditi Sharma
list.text: The rigorous training in Neuro-rehab helped me immensely. I felt fully equipped to open my own clinic right after getting my degree and completing the internship.

list.name: Siddharth Jain
list.text: The Cardio-pulmonary module and ICU rotations during my internship prepared me for the high-pressure environment of corporate hospitals. The faculty's guidance was invaluable.

m-pharma.title: Master of Pharmacy (M.Pharma)
m-pharma.eligibility: B.Pharma with minimum 55% marks from a PCI recognized university (GPAT qualified candidates preferred)
m-pharma.description: An advanced postgraduate program designed to cultivate elite research scientists, formulation experts, and clinical trial managers for the global pharmaceutical industry.
m-pharma.metaTitle: Master of Pharmacy (M.Pharma) | Health Sciences | CPU
m-pharma.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year M.Pharma degree at Career Point University. Focus on advanced drug formulation, clinical research, and pharmacology. Elite MNC placements.

hero.description: Pioneer the next medical breakthrough. Master advanced pharmaceutics, novel drug delivery systems, and clinical pharmacology to lead global pharma R&D.

accomplishments.heading: Department's Pharmaceutical R&D Impact
accomplishments.trustBadge: ✓ Approved by Pharmacy Council of India (PCI)

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Pharma Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Pharmacy (M.Pharma) is an elite postgraduate degree meant for B.Pharma graduates who want to move beyond general manufacturing and enter the highly lucrative fields of Drug Discovery, Clinical Trials, and specialized R&D.

The program is deeply research-oriented. Students spend their first year mastering advanced theoretical concepts and modern analytical instruments (like HPLC, GC, Mass Spectrometry). The entire second year is dedicated to independent scientific research, resulting in a master's thesis, patent filing, or a published paper in recognized scientific journals.

scope.body: An M.Pharma degree catapults a graduate into the senior echelons of the pharmaceutical industry. Graduates are heavily recruited by R&D departments of top MNCs (Sun Pharma, Cipla, Pfizer) as Research Scientists, Formulation R&D Managers, Clinical Data Managers, and Pharmacovigilance Experts. It is also the requisite qualification for taking up teaching positions in Pharmacy colleges or pursuing a Ph.D. in Pharmaceutical Sciences globally.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in clinical research and drug innovation

curriculum.subtitle: An advanced PCI-structured curriculum for R&D excellence
curriculum.introNote: The M.Pharma curriculum shifts the focus from learning existing drugs to discovering, formulating, and testing new ones.
curriculum.outroNote: The final year is exclusively dedicated to a Master's Dissertation (Research Project) conducted in our advanced labs or in collaboration with a pharma MNC.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year M.Pharma program follows the strict framework of the PCI. Semester 1 and 2 involve intense coursework and practical labs focusing on modern analytical techniques and specialized subjects. Semester 3 and 4 are entirely research-driven, where students work on a novel drug formulation or clinical study, culminating in a thesis defense.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Modern Pharmaceutical Analytical Techniques, Drug Delivery Systems, Modern Pharmaceutics, Regulatory Affairs, Pharmaceutics Practical I. Total Credits: 26

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Molecular Pharmaceutics (Nano Tech and Targeted DDS), Advanced Biopharmaceutics & Pharmacokinetics, Computer Aided Drug Delivery System, Cosmetic and Cosmeceuticals, Pharmaceutics Practical II. Total Credits: 26

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Research Methodology and Biostatistics, Journal Club, Discussion / Presentation (Proposal Presentation), Research Work (Phase I). Total Credits: 21

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Journal Club, Research Work (Phase II), Discussion / Final Presentation, Thesis Defense and Viva-Voce. Total Credits: 20

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through GPAT scores / CPU Entrance Exam, followed by Personal Interview.

notes: Students holding a valid GPAT scorecard are eligible for maximum scholarships and AICTE stipends.
Merit scholarship is offered based on B.Pharma graduation marks.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special research grants are provided for outstanding thesis projects.
scholarships.subtitle: Scholarship for Session 2026-27

reasons.desc: M.Pharma is the gateway to highly paid R&D roles in pharma MNCs, moving you out of the factory floor and into the innovation labs.
reasons.title: Elite R&D Careers

reasons.desc: It is the mandatory qualification if you wish to pursue a career in academics as an Assistant Professor in Pharmacy colleges.
reasons.title: Academic & Teaching Roles

reasons.desc: The curriculum focuses heavily on Clinical Trials and Pharmacovigilance, the two fastest-growing outsourcing sectors in Indian healthcare.
reasons.title: Clinical Research Focus

reasons.desc: The entire second year is dedicated to a master's thesis, allowing you to publish research papers and file patents before graduating.
reasons.title: Publish & Patent

reasons.desc: M.Pharma serves as the perfect foundational step for those wishing to pursue a Ph.D. globally.
reasons.title: Pathway to Ph.D.

items.a: Candidates must hold a Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma) degree from a PCI-approved institution with a minimum of 55% aggregate marks. Non-pharmacy graduates (B.Sc.) are NOT eligible.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can a B.Sc. student apply?

items.a: M.Pharma is a 2-year (4 semester) postgraduate program. The tuition fee is ₹55,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: Yes! Candidates who have qualified the GPAT (Graduate Pharmacy Aptitude Test) are given high preference during admission, are eligible for 100% tuition scholarships, and can claim AICTE stipends.
items.q: Does a GPAT score help in admission or scholarships?

items.a: B.Pharma covers general pharmacy across all subjects. M.Pharma is a highly specialized, research-intensive degree focused on one specific domain (like Pharmaceutics or Pharmacology) aiming to produce R&D scientists.
items.q: What is the difference between B.Pharma and M.Pharma?

items.a: Yes, the entire second year (Semesters 3 & 4) is dedicated to an independent research project. You must submit and defend a Master's Thesis to obtain the degree.

items.a: Graduates are hired as Formulation Scientists, Clinical Research Associates (CRAs), Pharmacovigilance Executives, Regulatory Affairs Managers, and Assistant Professors. Top recruiters include Sun Pharma, Dr. Reddy's, and Biocon.
items.q: What kind of corporate jobs will I get after M.Pharma?

items.a: Yes, completing an M.Pharma is the standard and required stepping stone to enroll in a Ph.D. program in Pharmaceutical Sciences.
items.q: Can I pursue a Ph.D. after completing M.Pharma?

mpt.title: Master of Physiotherapy (MPT)
mpt.eligibility: Bachelor of Physiotherapy (BPT) with minimum 55% marks and completed 6-month mandatory internship
mpt.description: An advanced clinical specialization degree designed to create expert physiotherapists with deep knowledge in Orthopedics, Neurology, or Sports Rehabilitation.
mpt.metaTitle: Master of Physiotherapy (MPT) | Health Sciences | CPU
mpt.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year MPT degree at Career Point University. Specialize in Orthopedics, Neurology, or Sports Rehab. Advanced clinical training and hospital placements.

hero.description: Become an elite rehabilitation expert. Master advanced diagnostic skills, evidence-based physical therapy, and lead independent clinical practices.

overview.subtitle: What is the MPT Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Physiotherapy (MPT) is a premier postgraduate program for BPT graduates seeking to become elite subject matter experts. General physiotherapy is no longer enough in modern medicine; hospitals and patients demand highly specialized therapists for complex conditions.

This program shifts the focus from basic treatment to advanced diagnostics, evidence-based practice, and clinical research. Depending on their chosen major (Orthopedics, Neurology, Sports, or Cardio), students spend their time mastering complex therapeutic maneuvers, publishing clinical research, and treating advanced cases in corporate hospital ICUs and dedicated OPDs.

scope.body: An MPT degree significantly elevates a physiotherapist's clinical authority and earning potential. Specialized MPT graduates are highly demanded as Heads of Rehabilitation Departments in corporate hospitals (Apollo, Max, Medanta), Lead Sports Physiotherapists for national athletic teams, and Ergonomic Consultants for MNCs. It is also the mandatory qualification for academic roles (Assistant Professor) in medical colleges and is highly respected for setting up premium, specialized independent private clinics.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in specialized clinical rehabilitation

curriculum.subtitle: An advanced clinical curriculum for rehabilitation mastery
curriculum.introNote: The MPT curriculum is divided into a foundation year focusing on advanced medical sciences, followed by a deeply specialized second year.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory clinical dissertation (research thesis) based on live patient data is required to complete the degree.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year MPT program is highly demanding. Year 1 builds upon advanced assessment skills, research methodology, and complex biomechanics applicable to all branches. Year 2 is exclusively dedicated to the student's chosen clinical specialization, extensive hospital postings, and an independent research dissertation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Basic Medical Sciences (Anatomy & Physiology applied to PT), Advanced Biomechanics & Kinesiology, Exercise Physiology, Clinical Observation & Assessment. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Research Methodology & Biostatistics, Advanced Electrotherapy & Diagnostics, Pedagogy in Physiotherapy, Evidence-Based Practice, Seminar & Case Presentation. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Specialization Paper I (Clinical Conditions & Assessment), Specialization Paper II (Physiotherapeutic Interventions), Advanced Clinical Posting I, Dissertation Synopsis Submission. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Specialization Paper III (Recent Advances & Surgeries), Advanced Clinical Posting II, Dissertation (Research Work & Thesis Defense), Final Viva-Voce. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Available Specializations
accordionSections.content: Students can choose to major in: 1. Orthopedics & Manual Therapy, 2. Neurology & Psychosomatic Disorders, 3. Sports Medicine & Rehabilitation, 4. Cardio-Pulmonary Sciences.

admissionCriteria.text: Admission based on BPT graduation marks, followed by Personal Interview / CPU Entrance Test.

notes: Merit scholarship is offered exclusively based on overall BPT graduation marks.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special considerations for students who have published research papers during their BPT internship.
If a student fails to maintain the required clinical/academic standards, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

reasons.desc: An MPT makes you a subject matter expert (e.g., Sports Physiotherapist), allowing you to charge premium consultation fees in independent practice.
reasons.title: Premium Independent Practice

reasons.desc: It is the mandatory qualification mandated by the UGC and medical councils if you wish to become a Professor in any Physiotherapy college.
reasons.title: Academic & Teaching Career

reasons.desc: Gain expertise in advanced therapeutic interventions like Dry Needling, Cupping, and Manual Therapy (Maitland/Mulligan concepts).
reasons.title: Advanced Treatment Skills

reasons.desc: The rigorous focus on Biostatistics and Research Methodology allows you to publish clinical papers and contribute to global medical science.
reasons.title: Clinical Research Focus

reasons.desc: Corporate hospitals prefer hiring specialized MPT graduates to head their specific Neuro, Ortho, or ICU rehabilitation departments.
reasons.title: Head Corporate Rehab Units

msc-public-health.title: M.Sc. in Public Health
msc-public-health.eligibility: Graduation in Life Sciences, Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Allied Health, or related fields with minimum 50% marks
msc-public-health.description: An advanced postgraduate program designed to build global health leaders, epidemiologists, and policy makers equipped to solve large-scale community health challenges.
msc-public-health.metaTitle: M.Sc. in Public Health | Health Sciences | CPU
msc-public-health.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year M.Sc. in Public Health at Career Point University. Master epidemiology, health policies, and biostatistics. Top placements in WHO, NGOs, and Govt. health sectors.

hero.description: Protect populations. Prevent diseases. Master the science of epidemiology, health economics, and global healthcare policies to create a healthier world.

accomplishments.heading: Department's Global Health Accomplishments & Impact
accomplishments.trustBadge: ✓ Aligned with Global Public Health Standards

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Sc. Public Health Programme?
paragraphs: The M.Sc. in Public Health is a multi-disciplinary postgraduate program that shifts the medical focus from treating individual patients to preventing diseases and promoting health at a population level. In a post-pandemic world, this is one of the most critical and highly funded fields globally.

The curriculum trains students to track disease outbreaks (Epidemiology), analyze large-scale health data (Biostatistics), and design effective health interventions. Students work on real-world community health projects, partnering with government health departments and international NGOs to understand grassroots healthcare challenges and formulate impactful policies.

scope.body: Public Health professionals are the architects of a nation's healthcare system. Graduates are highly sought after by International Organizations (WHO, UNICEF, Red Cross), Government Health Ministries (NHM), Corporate CSR divisions, and global Health Consultancies. Lucrative roles include Epidemiologist, Public Health Policy Advisor, Health Data Analyst, and Community Program Director. Furthermore, it is the perfect launchpad for pursuing a Ph.D. in Global Health or Epidemiology.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in global health and epidemiology

curriculum.subtitle: A globally aligned curriculum for health leadership
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum is designed to build strong analytical, research, and policy-making skills applicable to real-world health crises.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory field-based Public Health Dissertation and an internship with a recognized NGO or health agency are required.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year M.Sc. Public Health program is intensive and data-driven. Year 1 covers the core pillars of public health: Epidemiology, Biostatistics, and Environmental Health. Year 2 focuses on Health Economics, Global Health Policies, and culminates in a heavy, field-based research dissertation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles & Practice of Public Health, Basic Epidemiology, Biostatistics for Public Health, Environmental & Occupational Health, Social & Behavioral Health Sciences. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Advanced Epidemiology, Health Systems and Policies, Demography & Population Sciences, Communicable & Non-Communicable Diseases, Health Promotion & Communication. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Health Economics & Financing, Public Health Nutrition, Research Methodology & Ethics, Elective I (e.g., Maternal & Child Health), Dissertation Synopsis. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Global Health & Disaster Management, Elective II (e.g., Health Analytics), Mandatory NGO/Agency Internship, Master's Dissertation (Field Research & Defense). Total Credits: 24

admissionFee.subtitle: Transparent fee structure for M.Sc. Public Health

admissionCriteria.text: Admission based on Graduation marks, followed by Personal Interview / CPU Entrance Test.

notes: Special flat scholarships are available for students holding medical degrees (MBBS, BDS, BAMS) shifting to Public Health.
Merit scholarship is offered based on graduation marks.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special research grants are provided for outstanding community health thesis projects.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the Public Health programme

items.a: Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (B.Sc., B.Pharma, BPT, MBBS, BDS, BAMS, Nursing, or related allied health fields) with a minimum of 50% marks. Medical professionals looking to shift into health policy highly prefer this course.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can a B.Sc. student apply?

items.a: It is a 2-year (4 semester) postgraduate program. The tuition fee is ₹35,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: MHA focuses purely on running the operations and business of a single hospital. Public Health focuses on the health of entire populations, tracking diseases, and making national or global health policies (like vaccination drives).
items.q: What is the difference between MHA and M.Sc. Public Health?

items.a: Graduates are hired as Epidemiologists, Health Data Analysts, Public Health Advisors, and Program Managers. Top recruiters include WHO, UNICEF, State Health Ministries (NHM), and major NGOs like the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after M.Sc. Public Health?

items.a: Yes, completing an independent, field-based research dissertation (thesis) involving community health data is a mandatory requirement to obtain the Master's degree.
items.q: Is a research project or thesis mandatory?

items.a: Yes, students must complete an internship or field attachment with a recognized NGO, government health agency, or corporate CSR division during their final semester.
items.q: Is field work or an internship compulsory?

items.a: Yes, completing an M.Sc. in Public Health is the standard requirement if you wish to pursue a Ph.D. in Epidemiology, Health Economics, or Global Health.
items.q: Can I pursue a Ph.D. after completing this course?

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from graduate to global health expert.

years.contentDesc: The first year builds a rigorous foundation in epidemiology, biostatistics, and understanding how the environment affects community health.
The second year focuses on health economics, drafting health policies, and executing an independent field-based Master's Dissertation.

list.name: Dr. Siddharth Jain
list.text: The rigorous training in SPSS and Biostatistics during my first year was the exact skill set required to crack the technical interview at WHO.

list.name: Aditi Sharma
list.text: The mandatory field dissertation forced me to step out of the classroom and understand grassroots health issues. It was a transformative experience.

list.name: Dr. Vikram Singh
list.text: Moving from clinical dentistry to public health was the best decision. CPU's curriculum gave me the global perspective needed to consult for government health projects.

title.main: Healthcare Experts Making a Global Impact

community.label: Community Health
description: The School of Health Sciences believes that medical education goes beyond the classroom. Our students are actively involved in improving community health and well-being.

We regularly organize free diagnostic camps, blood donation drives, public health awareness campaigns, and free physiotherapy rehabilitation sessions for the underprivileged, teaching our students the core value of empathy in healthcare.

gallery.caption: Free Health and Vitals Checkup Camp

list.title: Advanced Pharmaceutics Lab
list.desc: Equipped with tablet punching machines, dissolution apparatus, and spectrophotometers for drug formulation training.

list.title: Physiotherapy OPD & Rehab Center
list.desc: A fully functional Outpatient Department featuring electrotherapy and exercise therapy units for live patient training.

list.title: Human Anatomy & Physiology Lab
list.desc: Featuring high-fidelity articulated human skeletons, anatomical 3D models, and advanced microscopes.

list.title: Dietetics & Nutrition Kitchen
list.desc: A modern clinical kitchen lab designed for students to prepare and analyze therapeutic diets for patients.

list.name: Aditi Sharma
list.text: The rigorous training in the Pharmaceutics lab made the transition to the industry extremely smooth. The campus placement cell is incredibly supportive.

list.name: Vikram Singh
list.text: Working directly with patients in the university's Physiotherapy OPD gave me the clinical confidence I needed. It's the best hands-on experience one could ask for.

list.name: Siddharth Jain
list.text: The curriculum perfectly balances human biology with food science. Creating live therapeutic diet plans in the lab prepared me for my hospital role.
